description: This golden hay-colored wine opens in a complex floral arrangement of honeysuckle and apple blossom before unleashing a weave of apricot and lychee fruit. The acidity is tart and high-toned.
title: Tessier Winery 2016 Catie's Corner Viognier (Russian River Valley)

description: The merest touch of passion fruit streaks the lemony, fresh nose. Although the palate seems a little dilute, it is light, fresh and finishes on a lingering lemon note.
title: Skoff 2016 Eichberger Sauvignon Blanc (Südsteiermark)

description: This starts off shy, but with time allows aromas of delicate peach, cherry and orange blossom to come through in the bouquet. Pleasant ripe-fruit flavors unfold on the clean, well-balanced palate, with lingering notes of peach pit and orange peel on the finish.
title: Les Vignerons de Florensac 2016 Rosé été Rosé (Côtes de Thau)

description: The nose is subdued but has a floral aspect. The palate, while fresh, slender and dry, seems to have a bitter element that gets in the way of the clean citrus notes. There is some concentration and a zesty lemony finish.
title: Domaine G. Metz 2016 Vieilles Vignes Riesling (Alsace)

description: Apple aromas set up a chunky palate that tastes of lightly oxidized apple and peach. It finishes solid but basic.
title: Verum 2016 Chardonnay (Alto Valle del Río Negro)

description: Intensely peachy from nose to finish, this sprightly semisweet sip offers loads of juicy fruity enjoyment. It's easy going and slightly candied on the palate but finishes crisply.
title: Leonard Kreusch 2016 Weinkellerei Kabinett White (Rheinhessen)

description: This blend of Fernão Pires, Arinto, Sauvignon Blanc and, unusually, Marsanne is ripe, with a deliciously perfumed character. Acidity and bright fruit give crispness that contrasts with the rich texture. Drink from 2018.
title: Quinta da Alorna 2016 Vinho Branco White (Tejo)

description: Made entirely from Grenache, this wine is whole-cluster fermented and aged in stainless steel. It's a dry, light expression that's pretty on the nose and palate, with inviting strawberry notes alongside hints of lemon and orange. It offers refreshing acidity alongside a meaty body and wet-stone minerality.
title: Anaba 2016 Rosé of Grenache (Sonoma Valley)

description: The grounded, earthy roundness of oak is immediately apparent on the nose, adding floral notes of hay and fresh field mushroom. On the pliable palate, aromatic, pale apple notes play with a touch of citrus and evocative hayflower notes. The body is light, with crisp freshness and a little midpalate texture. It finishes clean and bright.
title: Prieler 2016 Sinner Chardonnay (Burgenland)

description: A slightly earthy note colors the apple and lemon aromas on the nose. The palate shows lots of freshness and has a tart, light and dry body. The finish is lemony and fresh. Drink while aromas are expressive.
title: Joseph Cattin 2016 Riesling (Alsace)

description: This concentrated, herbal wine has a good balance between the great fruit and the tangy, zesty texture. With its fruity character and spicy aftertaste, it's already a pleasure to drink.
title: Château le Grand Verdus 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Faint notes of red currant and rhubarb appear on the nose. The palate is fresh and light, combining lemon and berry freshness with earthier notes. This is slender, dry and very refreshing.
title: Domaine Fernand Engel 2016 Pinot Noir Rosé (Alsace)

description: Aged for three months on its lees in small clay amphoras, this wine has a juicy, fruity character. The red fruits and crisp acidity are impressive and fascinating. Its texture and tannins suggest further aging, so drink from 2018.
designation: Herdade de São Miguel Art.Terra Amphora Tinto
title: Casa Agricola Alexandre Relvas 2016 Herdade de São Miguel Art.Terra Amphora Tinto Red (Alentejano)

description: This rosé is very Provencal in expression, with restrained aromas of orange mousse, nectarine, rose petals and wet cement on the nose. The palate is clean and crisp with a rainy concrete-like grip, showing slightly sour citrus and a twist of stone fruit on the sip.
title: Tercero 2016 Rosé of Mourvèdre (Santa Barbara County)

description: Minty berry aromas settle well and hold up to airing. This everyday Merlot is more than competent on the palate. Berry and spice flavors finish chewy, with standard ripeness and follow through.
title: Cuyen 2016 Merlot (Central Valley)

description: A candy-pink-colored wine, this is crisp, fruity and off dry. There is a touch of caramel that fills out the otherwise acidic and red-currant-flavored palate. The wine is ready to drink.
title: Quinta das Arcas 2016 Arca Nova Rosé (Vinho Verde)

description: Proof that that a pink wine can be as serious as it is delicious, this copper-tinged wine from James MacPhail offers almond, cured meat, raspberry, melon and lemon peel flavors. No shrinking violet here; it's at once crisp, fleshy and fully expressive.
title: Tongue Dancer 2016 Putnam Vineyard Rosé of Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Tan in color, with uncomplicated but friendly melon and apple aromas, this feels electric on the palate due to high acidity. The mix of citrus and stone-fruit flavors is lean, tight and lasting on the finish.
title: Ressò 2016 Old Vines Garnacha Blanca (Catalunya)

description: This fruity wine has crisp acidity and lemon flavors. It is tangy, zesty and lightly perfumed from the Verdelho in the blend. The Sauvignon Blanc adds its own tropical fruit note. Drink the wine now.
title: Cortes de Cima 2016 Chaminé Branco White (Alentejano)

description: Winemaker Kevin Riley's fascination with resurrecting the Mission grape vines on his property is starting to pay off. This pink, which includes 18% Zinfandel and 7% Grenache, shows ripe watermelon and strawberry aromas. Red plum and red apple flavors show on the palate, enlivened by mandarin orange accents.
title: Proulx 2016 Willow Creek Farm Rosé of Mission (Paso Robles)

description: Fermented and aged entirely in stainless steel, this is a subtly floral wine that flirts with apple blossom and jasmine scents on the nose. On the palate, expect creamy apricot, orange, lime and vanilla cookie flavors in a complex and layered way.
title: Ampelos 2016 Ampelos Vineyard Viognier (Santa Barbara County)

description: Light and bright, this is a perfumed wine. Its very present acidity is underpinned by spice, red-currant fruits and a tangy, tangerine aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Château le Grand Verdus 2016 Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: This mild, plump and basic Chardonnay leads with peach blossom, yeast and cured ham scents. Its melon, peach and apricot flavors hollow out on a mild, yeasty tasting finish.
title: Enate 2016 234 Chardonnay (Somontano)

description: Fragrant and nicely detailed, this sends up clover and apple blossom scents. Apple-infused flavors follow, with touches of peach and spice.
title: Elk Cove 2016 Pinot Gris (Willamette Valley)

description: This beautifully balanced wine has refreshing red fruit flavors cut by intense acidity and orange zest and lemon accents. Tangy, mineral and certainly worth aging, it's ready to drink but will be even better in summer 2018.
title: Clos Teddi 2016 Grande Cuvée Rosé (Patrimonio)

description: Made from Syrah, Grenache, Mourvèdre and Counoise grapes grown at the producer's 95-acre estate, this is a dry, simple wine with tart yet ripe strawberry and peach notes.
title: Lasseter 2016 Enjoué Estate Grown Rosé (Sonoma Valley)

description: Candied cherry and tangerine are welcome flavors in this crisp, dry and exceptionally light and easy-drinking wine. It's bright and long-lasting in refreshment.
designation: Eye of the Toad Dry Rosé of
title: Toad Hollow 2016 Eye of the Toad Dry Rosé of Pinot Noir (Sonoma County)

description: When the slightly reductive and rubbery funk blows off about 10 minutes after opening this appellation blend, then come great aromas of rounded red apple, cut grass and wet rocks. There is a solid boost of acidity to the tip of the sip and a tantalizing structure, with flavors of Granny Smith apple and lime rinds. This is a full-bodied, even aggressive Sauv Blanc.
title: Brander 2016 Sauvignon Blanc

description: This clean, mild quaffer offers basic white-fruit aromas and a light-bodied, fresh palate. Apple and mild citrus flavors are short on the finish, which takes on an apricot flavor as it fades away.
title: Mariposa 2016 Chardonnay (San Juan)

description: This crisp, clean and zesty wine is lightly herbal and immediately appealing. It's full of citrus and gooseberry flavors and is ready to drink.
title: Vindivin 2016 La Belle Saison Sauvignon Blanc (Vin de France)

description: The aromas are bright, with notes of guava, pineapple and citrus. The palate is lightly spritzy with plenty of grapefruit flavors that keep the interest high.
title: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Ripe banana, coconut, mango and guava aromas make for a showy entry in this bottling, but citrusy freshness keeps it in check. The palate show a clean line of lemony acidity that sears into the finish, cutting through hints of more tropical fruit.
title: Fess Parker 2016 Chardonnay (Santa Barbara County)

description: Aromas of pear, cantaloupe and apple are followed by broad, dry fruit flavors, with a spritzy finish. There's not much complexity, but the wine goes down easy and is very varietal.
title: 14 Hands 2016 Pinot Gris (Washington)

description: This beauty of a sweet wine has a perfume like jasmine and honeysuckle blossoms and flavors like Anjou pears and lychees. It's very ripe, succulent and almost thick on the palate, but far from simply syrupy. The exotic flavors expand with each sip, and linger a long time on the finish.
title: Mt. Konocti Winery 2016 Un Doux Baiser Late Harvest Muscat Canelli (Clear Lake)

description: Full bodied and full flavored, this barrel-fermented dry wine is bold and oaky in aromas, rich with ripe apple and pear flavors and accented by nutmeg, butter and vanilla. The texture is smooth but supported by good acidity, and it has mouth-filling viscosity that encourages a long finish.
title: Monochrome 2016 Blasphemy Donnachadh Vineyard Chardonnay (California)

description: From the eastern side of the Napa Valley, this combines 84% Grenache with 16% Mourvèdre, harvesting the best clusters of the grapes specifically for this light, dry wine. The juice is left to ferment and rest in concrete egg and neutral French oak. Light-copper in color, it's perfumed in jasmine, with accents of white peach, lime and strawberry, light and ethereal with a backbone of focused acidity.
title: Kale 2016 McGah Vineyard Rosé (Rutherford)

description: A touch of yeast and sage emerges amidst lime peel scents on the nose of this wine. The palate reinforces those flavors, while white pepper and lime notes tingle around the edges. It's lean but concnetrated and finishes with such a clean and refreshing zip that you just want to sip more.
title: Stadt Krems 2016 Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: Though considered a rosé, Grenache Gris is actually its own grape, and a delicious one at that. Pale pink in color, this shows bubblegum, watermelon, rose petal, strawberry and cola spice on the nose. The palate is marked by boisterous, lemon-like acidity yet remains soft and welcoming, with sweet cotton candy touches.
title: Curran 2016 Grenache Gris Rosé (Santa Barbara County)

description: Stéphane Defraine's wine with its attractive fruitiness is well balanced, bright and crisp. It has a refreshing character, juicy with red-currant fruit and delicious acidity, making it a great summer apéritif wine.
title: Château de Fontenille 2016 Rosé (Bordeaux Rosé)

description: A touch of strawberry streaks through this wine's lemon scent. The palate is slender, easy and fresh, making for an uncomplicated summer drink with a dry, taut finish.
title: Familie Zull 2016 Lust & Laune Rosé (Niederösterreich)

description: Cutting crisp tangerine, peach and honeydew melons abound on the nose of this full-bodied textured Sauvignon Blanc. Sprightly lemon-lime acidity lends balance and edge, and a hint of bitterness on the finish lends elegant structure.
title: McCall 2016 Cuvée Nicola North Ridge Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc (North Fork of Long Island)

description: A touch of flinty reduction colors the nose, showing how young this wine still is. The palate, however, is fresh and sprightly in shape, but totally rich and concentrated in flavor. Svelte generosity shows in the very fluid midpalate texture and a lasting lemon finish.
title: Anton Bauer 2016 Gmörk Grüner Veltliner (Wagram)

description: This is a warm and ripe wine with a smooth caramel texture. That lies over the ripe red-berry fruits and balanced acidity. The result is a rounded wine that is ready to drink.
title: Georges Vigouroux 2016 Pigmentum Malbec Rosé (Côtes du Lot)

description: This is one of the more interesting rosés of the vintage, in part because it is almost as hay-colored as a white wine and doesn't adhere to the standard pink profile. Aromas of crushed Marcona almonds, sea salt and oak smoke show on the nose, and the acid-driven palate continues that nutty and smokey drive. It's intriguing and unique, though not particularly pink.
title: Daou 2016 Rosé (Paso Robles)

description: From a 40-plus-year-old vineyard, this peppery wine is dry, fruity and bold. It combines peach, apple and orange fruit flavors with a lusciously full-bodied mouthfeel that's also layered and well structured. Drink now and over the next decade.
title: Hyland 2016 Old Vine Riesling (McMinnville)

description: The wine comes from vines planted on a small island off the Provence coast. Its cool seaside climate has given a crisp red-currant flavored wine. Light and bright with lively acidity, the wine is textured with taut minerality. Drink now.
title: Domaine Perzinsky 2016 Les Forts de Porquerolles Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: From vines close to the Sainte-Victoire mountain, the wine is crisply textured, with tangy red currants. It is a bright stylish wine, finely balanced and lifted with acidity and fruitiness. Drink now.
title: Domaine Sainte Lucie 2016 MiP Made in Provence Classic Rosé (Côtes de Provence)

description: Solid aromas of white peach, gooseberry, sweet grass and lime leaf lead the bouquet of this charming wine. The medium-weight palate is cut by ample acidity, leading with ripe fruit flavors that transition to notes of pear skin and subtle gingery spice on the close. It's well balanced and offers good evolution through the sip. Drink now–2019.
title: Buitenverwachting 2016 Bayten Sauvignon Blanc (Constantia)

description: Fresh citrus and green-apple aromas lead to a healthy palate with just enough bracing acidity. Flavors of tangerine, passion fruit and grapefruit are bold and typical of Casablanca SB, while this tastes of lime, green herbs and scallion on a fit finish.
title: Matetic 2016 EQ Coastal Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: This value-priced low-alcohol wine has all the refreshing elements for a good rosé: light body, mild watermelon and peach flavors and a crisp texture. It has fresh acidity and good balance, too.
title: Bota Box 2016 Dry Rosé (California)

description: This crisp wine has plenty of acidity as well as lively fruits. The structure and texture are light and lifted, with a clean aftertaste. Drink now.
title: Château Lary 2016  Bordeaux Blanc

description: Ripe strawberries and crisp acidity blend together in this refreshing, crisp and fruity wine. Produced from Cabernet Franc, the wine is lightly perfumed and with a final shot of bright acidity. Drink now.
title: Domaine Dozon 2016 Rosé (Chinon)

description: This open, fruity wine is named after Cape Bojador in the western Sahara, which Portuguese sailors rounded in the 15th century on their way around Africa. The wine's red fruits and acidity are easy, ripe and with a juicy aftertaste.
title: Espaço Rural 2016 Bojador Red (Alentejano)

description: Crushed earth notes juxtapose ripe lime, tangerine and melon in this refreshing easy-drinking Sauvignon Blanc. Medium bodied with a crisp tonic mouthfeel and brisk acidity, it's an appealing apéritif and summer quaffer.
title: Wölffer 2016 Sauvignon Blanc (Long Island)

description: This is a smooth, ripe style of Sauvignon Blanc. It hints at mango and pineapple as well as more crisply citrus fruit and shows an herbal, green fruit character on the finish. Drink now.
title: Lionel Osmin & Cie 2016 La Réserve Sauvignon Blanc (Vin de France)

description: Exotic fruit and herbaceous aromas lift out of the glass. On the bright, simple palate, zesty acidity underscores juicy pineapple and grapefruit notes.
title: Andrero 2016 Grillo (Terre Siciliane)

description: A pale colored rosé, light and attractively crisp. Soft red fruits come through the gentle texture to give a wine that is ripe, fruity and ready to drink.
title: Casa Santos Lima 2016 Lab Rosé (Lisboa)

description: Grapy aromas of black cherry are young and fruity but unsophisticated. This everyday Tempranillo is more superficial than deep in build. Sweet as well as peppery black-fruit flavors finish mild.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla
title: Dominio de Punctum 2016 Lobetia Tempranillo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla)

description: There's a medicinal cherry hint to the nose of this Syrah-based rosé, with additional notes of ripe peach and melon. It's fleshy and full-fruited on the palate, with just enough acidity to keep the ripeness in check.
title: Les Costières de Pomerols 2016 Beau Vignac Rosé (Pays d'Oc)

description: This crisp wine is as light as a feather—fruity and perfumed. The fruit came from the highest altitude of the quinta, resulting in the wine's cool character and considerable acidity. Drink now.
title: Quinta do Vallado 2016 Vallado Touriga Nacional Rosé (Douro)

description: It's hard to believe it's possible to buy this quality rosé for so little money. Mouthwatering scents of raspberry, stone fruit and crushed stone emerge from the glass, followed by rich, ripe fruit flavors balanced by just-enough citrusy acidity. It's full-bodied and plush, yet vibrant and long on the finish.
title: Domaine des Carteresses 2016  Tavel
